steps.assistant_text: libultra osRecvMesg (kernel_002, IDO 7.1 -O1). Recognized from the asm shape (__osDisableInt/__osRestoreInt interrupt bracket, validCount==0 yield loop, ring-index `% msgCount` with the IDO signed-mod div0/INT_MIN break-pair, __osPopThread/osStartThread fullqueue drain). Reconstructed directly from libreultra src/os/recvmesg.c with the 1080 OSMesgQueue offsets (0x4 fullqueue, 0x8 validCount, 0xC first, 0x10 msgCount, 0x14 msg[]) and Thread.state at 0x10 (u16, set 8 = OS_STATE_WAITING). `register s32 saveMask` pins the int-save into s0 across the loop; `mq->fullqueue->next` (fullqueue is a list head whose first field is next) reproduces the `lw 0x4; lw 0x0; beqz` drain guard; func_8000A110(func_80003E54(&mq->fullqueue)) is the single-arg osStartThread of the popped thread. Compiled .text is byte-identical to the target (78 insns, 0 non-reloc diffs). The real function occupies 0x140 (2 trailing 16-byte-alignment nops at 0x5D18/0x5D1C before func_80004D20 at 0x5D20); those nops are spliced back via a pad sidecar (#pragma GLOBAL_ASM func_80004BE0_pad.s, genuine all-zero alignment padding) so the .o/ROM layout is preserved. Full `make clean && make` produces a ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp clean, checksum OK).

metadata.verification: Full make clean && make produces ROM byte-identical to baserom (cmp clean, md5 OK). func_80004BE0 .text byte-identical to target (78 insns, 0 diffs); 2 trailing 16-byte alignment nops spliced via func_80004BE0_pad.s GLOBAL_ASM sidecar. Source: libultra osRecvMesg (libreultra src/os/recvmesg.c).
